message:
test(core): property-based tests for exponential histogram invariants (wa-2q24)

23 proptest properties verifying:
- Count conservation: count == sum(bucket_details)
- Percentile monotonicity: p(x) <= p(y) when x <= y, p50 <= p90 <= p99
- Mean within [min, max] bounds
- Min/max tracking matches actual extremes
- Sum accuracy matches manual accumulation
- mean == sum / count identity
- record_n equivalence with n individual record() calls
- Clear resets all state completely, reuse works after clear
- Merge: count/sum conservation, matches single-histogram recording
- Merge with empty histogram is identity
- Stats serde JSON roundtrip preserves all fields
- Empty histogram returns None for all optional accessors
- Custom base histogram count conservation
- Non-positive values classified as underflow
- Large values classified as overflow
- Bucket boundaries monotonically increasing
- num_buckets matches construction parameters

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -326,6 +326,7 @@
 {"id":"wa-2oph2","title":"SIMD-accelerated pane output processing — vectorized scanning and compression","description":"# SIMD-Accelerated Output Processing

## Skills: /alien-artifact-coding, /extreme-software-optimization

## Problem
200 panes producing output simultaneously. Each pane output must be scanned for patterns, compressed for storage, and forwarded to observers. At scale, this is CPU-bound.

## Solution: Vectorized processing pipeline

### 1. SIMD pattern scanning
Use SIMD (AVX2/NEON) to scan pane output for:
- Newline boundaries (for line counting)
- ANSI escape sequence detection (for stripping)
- Pattern triggers (e.g., error keywords, completion markers)
- UTF-8 validation

Reference: /alien-artifact-coding for formal correctness proof of SIMD scanning.

### 2. Vectorized compression
- Use zstd with SIMD acceleration for scrollback compression
- Batch multiple pane outputs into single compression calls
- Pre-trained dictionary for terminal output (high compression ratio)
- **Plugs into wa-2ahu0 memory engine**: provides the compression primitives that the memory pressure engine calls for scrollback compression/eviction

### 3. Zero-copy output pipeline
- mmap-based buffer for pane output capture
- Process in-place without allocation
- Only copy when observer needs a snapshot

### 4. Benchmarks (/extreme-software-optimization)
Must profile and prove:
- Pattern scan throughput: >2GB/s per core
- Compression throughput: >500MB/s per core
- Zero-copy reduces allocation by >90% vs current

## Tests
- **Criterion benchmarks** for pattern scanning (varying output patterns: plain text, ANSI-heavy, binary-heavy)
- **Criterion benchmarks** for compression (terminal output corpus with pre-trained dictionary)
- Throughput test: 200 panes producing 10MB/s each, verify no backpressure
- SIMD correctness: identical results to scalar fallback on ALL inputs (property-based testing with proptest)
- **Platform tests**: AVX2 on x86_64, NEON on aarch64 (Apple Silicon), scalar fallback everywhere

## Acceptance criteria
- SIMD scanning >2GB/s throughput
- Compression >500MB/s throughput with >5:1 ratio on terminal output
- Zero-copy pipeline reduces allocations measurably
- Scalar fallback for non-SIMD platforms
- Formally verified correctness (/alien-artifact-coding)

## Test Framework Requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks**: Must demonstrate >4x improvement over scalar baseline for all SIMD paths:
  - Newline scanning: benchmark SIMD vs scalar on 1KB, 64KB, 1MB, 16MB buffers. SIMD must achieve >4x speedup.
  - ANSI escape detection: benchmark on ANSI-heavy terminal output (50%+ escape sequences). SIMD must achieve >4x speedup.
  - UTF-8 validation: benchmark against std::str::from_utf8 baseline. SIMD must achieve >4x speedup on valid UTF-8 and equivalent error detection on invalid.
  - Compression: benchmark zstd with and without SIMD. Measure both throughput and compression ratio.
- **Proptest for SIMD/scalar equivalence**: For every SIMD-accelerated function, generate random byte sequences and verify the SIMD result is bit-identical to the scalar fallback:
  - Newline positions must match exactly
  - ANSI escape boundaries must match exactly
  - UTF-8 validation accept/reject decisions must match exactly
  - Compression/decompression roundtrip must produce identical output

## macOS Compatibility (Apple Silicon)
- **NEON on Apple Silicon**: All Apple Silicon Macs (M1/M2/M3/M4) support NEON SIMD. Use `std::arch::aarch64` intrinsics with `#[cfg(target_arch = "aarch64")]`.
- **Test both AVX2 and NEON paths**: CI must test AVX2 on x86_64 Linux/macOS and NEON on aarch64 macOS. Use runtime feature detection (`is_x86_feature_detected!("avx2")`) on x86_64 and compile-time `#[cfg(target_arch = "aarch64")]` for NEON (always available on Apple Silicon).
- **Scalar fallback**: Always available for platforms with neither AVX2 nor NEON. The scalar path is the reference implementation for correctness testing.
- **Benchmark all three paths**: Criterion benchmarks should include NEON results on Apple Silicon alongside AVX2 results on x86_64, with scalar as the baseline for both.

## Cross-References
- **wa-3bja.3** (Ghostty I/O pipeline): Ghostty achieves extreme I/O performance using SIMD-accelerated terminal parsing in Zig. This bead applies the same philosophy in Rust — study Ghostty's approach to vectorized escape sequence parsing and adapt the techniques. The two beads share performance targets and may share benchmark infrastructure.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:09.705391Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:47:03.879984Z"}
 {"id":"wa-2pii","title":"Incremental scan storage + report format","description":"## What
Persist scan checkpoints and define report schema.

## Why
Incremental scans avoid reprocessing huge datasets every time.

## How
- Store last scanned segment ID + report metadata
- Define report schema (counts, timestamps, pattern IDs)

## Success Criteria
- Reports are versioned and forward-compatible
- Resuming a scan skips previously scanned segments","notes":"Fixed baseline compile/test issues: added NotificationConfig.email in test initializers (config.rs/webhook.rs), replaced rsplit_whitespace, adjusted redaction/secret-scan tests to use valid long sk- key. All checks passing now (fmt/check/clippy/test).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:16:43.636350245Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:34:51.008429-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:34:51.008434-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2pii","depends_on_id":"wa-5wge","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-2pir","title":"Web server tests: /health + basic endpoint contract (feature web)","description":"# Task: Web server tests (feature web)

## Goal
Prevent the optional web server from silently rotting.

The web server is not on the critical path, but when enabled it must be reliable, safe, and schema-stable.

## Test scope
- Runs under `cargo test --features web`.
- Fully offline (no real WezTerm required).

## Required test cases
- Lifecycle:
  - start server on `--port 0`
  - hit `/health`
  - shut down cleanly

- Contract:
  - `/health` schema is parseable and stable
  - `/panes`, `/events`, `/search` responses are:
    - valid JSON
    - stable ordering where applicable
    - redacted (no raw secrets)

- Safety:
  - default bind is localhost
  - public bind requires explicit `--dangerous-bind-any`

## Observability in tests
- Capture request logs/spans and assert a few key fields exist (method/path/status/latency).
- Assert that sensitive values do not appear in logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo test --features web` includes at least:
  - `/health` responds
  - endpoint response bodies are schema-parseable
  - redaction + bind-default invariants are enforced


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Add a negative test that attempts `--dangerous-bind-any` without explicit confirmation (if applicable) and ensure it is rejected.
  - Add an assertion that there are no write/mutation endpoints registered.

 {"id":"wa-2q5r","title":"FTUI-01.1.a ADR: one-writer rule adaptation and terminal ownership rationale","description":"## Background
FrankenTUI's one-writer terminal ownership model must be translated into wa-specific runtime boundaries before implementation tasks proceed.

## Deliverables
- explicit mapping from ftui ownership principles to wa modules/process boundaries
- decision table for allowed/forbidden write paths during UI-active and command-handoff states
- risk notes for migration edge cases (panic path, subprocess path, inline mode)

## Acceptance Criteria
- mapping is concrete enough to serve as an implementation contract for downstream runtime tasks
- at least one negative example per forbidden write path is documented
- document includes required evidence fields for later unit/E2E validation and logging checks.,","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:13:51.443783314Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T03:53:32.410431876Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T03:53:32.41036381Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2q5r","depends_on_id":"wa-2dlw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:13:51.475021131Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}

- Token validation and scope enforcement

## Success Criteria
- Tests cover invalid token, expired token, and scope mismatch","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:52.657753778Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:34:50.745682-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:34:50.745689-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2qg0","depends_on_id":"wa-1oey","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-2qg0","depends_on_id":"wa-3p06","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-2qyt","title":"FTUI-03.1 Introduce terminal session ownership abstraction aligned with ftui","description":"## Background\nCurrent TUI setup/teardown is tied to crossterm/ratatui lifecycle. We need a migration-safe ownership abstraction that matches ftui terminal session discipline.\n\n## Deliverables\n- terminal session lifecycle interface\n- raw mode / alt-screen / cleanup ownership model\n- integration points for command handoff and return\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- session ownership is singular and testable\n- teardown guarantees are explicit and verified.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderGrove","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:49.283003308Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:27:27.521898614Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:27:27.521835436Z","close_reason":"Implemented terminal_session module: TerminalSession trait, SessionPhase enum, SessionGuard RAII, CrosstermSession impl, MockTerminalSession. 12 tests. Clippy clean.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2qyt","depends_on_id":"wa-1brb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:49.299737512Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-2qyt","depends_on_id":"wa-8q4e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:16:19.162217282Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
